claims.claim: Utah State University rack-level PDU RFP awarded to low bidder at $57,900 against only one other bidder
claims.sourceTitle: Bid Results on Uu207787178 Rack Level Power Distribution Units
claims.excerpt: Intellivex Data Center Solutions is the apparent low bidder on the Utah State University's UU207787178 Rack Level Power Distribution Units solicitation, with a low bid of $57,900.00 USD. Bids were opened on October 30, 2025

claims.claim: Only one competing bid was recorded against the low bidder on the Utah rack-PDU RFP
claims.sourceTitle: Bid Results on Uu207787178 Rack Level Power Distribution Units
claims.excerpt: Additional bidders include: Malor & Company Inc: $113,331.30 USD

claims.claim: University of Utah PDU solicitation specified a sole vendor/model with explicit 'No substitutions' clause
claims.sourceTitle: Bid Results on Enlogic Power Distribution Units
claims.excerpt: Vendor must have the ability to provide the specific equipment listed below. No substitutions. Model: EN6952-0000-i Enlogic, iPDU (EN6 Series) Outlet Switched and Metered, 208VAC, 3-phase, 60 A

claims.claim: The sole-sourced Enlogic PDU RFP was awarded at $129,775.09
claims.sourceTitle: Bid Results on Enlogic Power Distribution Units
claims.excerpt: Intellivex Data Center Solutions is the apparent low bidder on the University of Utah - Purchasing's Enlogic Power Distribution Units solicitation, with a low bid of $129,775.09 USD. Bids were opened on June 5, 2025

claims.claim: Naval Research Laboratory issued a sources-sought notice for six modular PDUs as part of computer room infrastructure replacement
claims.excerpt: The Cyber Operations Division at the Naval Research Laboratory (NRL) located at 4555 Overlook Ave SW, Washington DC, has a requirement to install six (6) Modular Power Distribution Units (PDUs) as part of its ongoing efforts to replace antiquated computer room infrastructure

claims.claim: A federal small-business set-aside buy bundled 9 data center racks with 39 PDUs into a single procurement rather than a standalone PDU contest
claims.excerpt: This procurement is for 9 data center racks and 39 data center power distribution units (PDUs).

claims.claim: ZincFive's estimated annual revenue is currently $26.9M per year (growjo estimate, spans NiZn battery/UPS products, not PDU-specific)
claims.excerpt: ZincFive's estimated annual revenue is currently $26.9M per year.(i)

claims.claim: ZincFive SPAC merger values the company at approximately $752 million pro forma enterprise value
claims.sourceTitle: ZincFive bets on revenue record in US$752 million SPAC merger - Energy-Storage.News
claims.excerpt: The transaction values ZincFive at approximately US$752 million on a pro forma enterprise value basis.

claims.claim: ZincFive reports surpassing one gigawatt of mission-critical power solutions delivered and contracted across multiple continents
claims.sourceTitle: ZincFive announces 1GW of mission-critical data center power solutions delivered and contracted - DCD
claims.excerpt: ZincFive, the world leader in nickel-zinc (NiZn) battery-based solutions for immediate power applications, has surpassed one gigawatt (GW) in power delivered and contracted across multiple continents

claims.claim: Enlogic's estimated annual revenue is $5.0-25M per Owler company profile
claims.sourceTitle: Enlogic's Competitors, Revenue, Number of Employees, Funding, Acquisitions & News - Owler Company Profile

tensions.text: The obtainableFactor node's crossCheck claims 'open 2026 EU rack-PDU tenders keep 2-3% inside the band,' but the only concrete standalone PDU procurements found in this search were small (~$58K-$130K) US university/DoD bids with 1-2 bidders and one explicitly sole-sourced ('No substitutions'), not a body of open, contestable EU tenders supporting a 2-3% share.

A PDU procurement specifying a single named vendor/model with 'No substitutions' is direct evidence that some 'standalone' RFPs are not actually contestable, which would push the truly-contestable share of SAM below even the 1% Year-1 anchor rather than validating the 0.5-3% band as open competition.

The Unison federal buy bundling 9 racks with 39 PDUs into one award (rather than a standalone PDU RFP) illustrates the bundling-forecloses-bakeoffs mechanism this group was tasked to quantify, but no channel-split percentage was found to confirm how much total PDU volume this bundling pattern represents versus standalone contests.

absences: No count of contestable standalone rack/row-PDU RFPs over the last 24 months was found — only a handful of individual bid-result pages (2 Utah bids, 1 DoD sources-sought, 1 federal marketplace buy) with no aggregation into a pipeline count.
No win-rate data for any vendor across multiple PDU RFPs was found.
No cycle-time (RFP-to-award duration) data was found beyond single bid-open dates on isolated solicitations.
No capacity (deliverable volume/production capacity) data tied to PDU procurement pipelines was found.
No share-of-channel split (fraction of PDU volume moving through vendor bundles vs. standalone contests) was found or quantified anywhere in the results.

No first-year revenue-as-percentage-of-SAM figure was found for any comparable entrant (Enlogic, ZincFive) — only absolute revenue/valuation estimates untethered to a stated SAM, so the 'is 3% Year-1 share attainable' question posed by the second query remains unanswered.
No data distinguishing what fraction of the underlying SAM is even open to competitive bid versus locked via specification-narrowing ('No substitutions') clauses was found.
